Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: auto-close implemented issues on merge (#72)

## Problem

Generated implement PRs use `Implements #N` in the PR body. GitHub does
not treat `Implements` as an auto-closing keyword, so completed issues
stay open after the PR merges.

## Solution

Switch the generated PR body to `Closes #N` so issue state tracks merged
implementations automatically.

## Changes

- update `implement_issue()` PR body wording from `Implements` to
`Closes`
- add a regression test asserting the generated PR body contains the
auto-closing keyword

Co-authored-by: Colin Cheung <11246116+ColinCee@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore(state): update STATE.md and SUMMARY.md after GSD & Metaswarm setup

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

suppress auto-close for quotes inserted inside strings or before words (#491)

`closing_char_to_insert` was incorrectly auto-closing quotes in two
cases: when the insertion point is inside an already-closed quoted
string, and when it falls at the start of an existing word token.

## New suppression rules (ambiguous chars `"`, `'`, `` ` `` only)

- **Inside matched string**: if `just_inserted_pos` falls in `[open_end,
close_start]` of a matched `Quote`/`SingleQuote` pair, return `None`.
  ```
  buffer: "abcde"  cursor between b and c
  type '  →  "ab'cde"   (no auto-close, ' is literal content)
  ```

- **Before/within a word**: if a `Word` token's byte range contains
`just_inserted_pos`, return `None`.
  ```
  buffer: foo bar  cursor before bar
  type "  →  foo "bar   (not foo "bar")
  ```

Both checks are inserted after the unambiguous-opener early-returns
(`{`, `[`, `(`) and before the existing `has_unmatched_opener` check, so
they have no effect on bracket auto-closing.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: HalFrgrd <4559349+HalFrgrd@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add upgrade mode to codenook-init

When re-initializing a project that already has CodeNook:
- Preserve runtime data: task-board.json, memory/, config.json, reviews/
- Regenerate framework files: agent profiles, HITL scripts, engine
- Merge config.json: update version, add new keys, keep user settings
- Skip interactive prompts in upgrade mode (use existing config)
- Show preserved vs updated counts in completion summary

Three choices on existing installation:
  Upgrade (recommended) | Fresh install | Cancel

commit.message:
Two-phase workspace card animation: collapse then fade

Single AnimationController (420ms) with two Interval phases:

EXIT (ctrl 1.0 → 0.0):
  Phase 1 [1.0→0.5]: tree collapses via SizeTransition
  Phase 2 [0.5→0.0]: border/background fades out

ENTER (ctrl 0.0 → 1.0):
  Phase 1 [0.0→0.5]: border/background fades in
  Phase 2 [0.5→1.0]: tree expands via SizeTransition

Interval curves naturally handle reverse direction — no extra logic needed.
Delay in _WorkspaceListState updated to 420ms to match full exit duration.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: auto-maintained wiki — watcher + maintenance agent + lifecycle hooks (#256-258)

The wiki now self-maintains on every code change:

WikiWatcher (#256):
- FNV-1a file hashing, recursive directory walk
- Extension filtering (.rs .ts .py .go .md .json)
- Ignore patterns (node_modules, .git, target)
- Detects created/modified/deleted files via hash diff

WikiMaintenanceAgent (#257):
- Plans actions per change: create/update/delete pages
- Post-pass: rebuild index.md, run lint, append log.md
- execute_action dispatches each action type

WikiAutoTrigger (#258):
- on_session_start: take initial project snapshot
- on_agent_turn_complete: diff → plan → execute → update snapshot
- on_session_end: final maintenance pass
- force_maintenance: manual trigger
- Snapshot taken AFTER actions to prevent re-detection loops

Flow: edit code → agent turn completes → wiki auto-updates
No manual intervention needed.

Tests: 1015 → 1040 (25 new tests)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: trim area group players to selected level range

When a level bin filter was active, public and other area groups were
shown correctly, but each group still rendered all of its original
players. That made the counts and tooltips include characters outside
the selected range.

Update the filtered area groups to keep only matching characters and
recompute each group's count from the filtered list.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Detect and report inaccessible key bindings in `flyline key list` (#492)

`flyline key list` had no way to surface bindings that can never fire
due to priority shadowing. Two conflict patterns go undetected: a
`Scope::Any` binding at higher priority than a scoped binding for the
same key (Any is always active, so the scoped binding is unreachable),
and duplicate key codes within the same scope (only the highest-priority
one fires).

## Changes

- **`key_event_match_overlaps(a, b)`** — determines whether two
`KeyEventMatch` patterns can both be triggered by the same physical key
press; handles `Exact`/`Exact`, `AnyCharAndMods`/`AnyCharAndMods`, and
mixed cases
- **`binding_shadows(higher, lower)`** — encapsulates the two shadowing
rules (Any-over-scoped; same-scope duplicate)
- **`detect_binding_conflicts(user_bindings, remappings)`** — iterates
all bindings highest-priority-first (same order as `handle_key_event`)
and collects every `(key_display, inaccessible_action,
shadowing_action)` triple
- **`print_bindings_table`** — calls the detector and appends a `Key
Binding Conflicts:` section after the remappings block; `INACCESSIBLE:
<key>` is rendered as blinking white on red (`\x1b[5;37;41m`) when
stdout is a TTY

Example output with the default bindings (two `Esc` bindings in
`Scope::Any`):
```
Key Binding Conflicts:
  INACCESSIBLE: Esc  (any::toggle_mouse shadowed by any::escape_to_normal_mode)
  INACCESSIBLE: Super+Shift+Z  (any::undo shadowed by any::redo)
```

Unit tests cover all `key_event_match_overlaps` variants, user-defined
conflict scenarios, and validate that the default bindings produce the
expected conflicts.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: HalFrgrd <4559349+HalFrgrd@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: level range filter now shows all area types (public, raid, other)

Previously, the filter used g.quest?.level ?? g.level to match the
selected level range. Public areas and other areas have level: null,
so they were always excluded when any level bin was selected.

Fix: filter groups by whether any character in the group has a
total_level within the selected range, so all area types are shown
correctly when a level bin is clicked.

commit.message:
fix(overlay): constrain dropdown to field width, remove full-screen barrier

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Sequential workspace transition: fade-out old, then fade-in new

Previously the old card disappeared instantly and the new one faded in.
Now the transition is sequential:
1. User clicks workspace → _displayedActiveId stays on old, fadeOut=true
   → old card plays reverse animation (220ms)
2. After 220ms delay → _displayedActiveId switches to new workspace
   → new _ActiveWorkspaceCard mounts and plays forward animation

_WorkspaceList is extracted as a StatefulWidget that manages
_displayedActiveId with a Future.delayed gate during transitions.
_ActiveWorkspaceCard gains a fadeOut param that triggers ctrl.reverse().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add -readrate to per-track ffmpeg for near-realtime output

Instead of modifying MA server's -re flag globally (which would break
Spotify Connect and other realtime plugin sources), throttle our own
per-track ffmpeg input reading to ~1.1x with a 5-second initial burst.

This makes our PCM output arrive at near-realtime rate, compatible
with MA's -re flag on the outer ffmpeg, while eliminating startup
latency via the initial burst.

Applied to both _stream_track and prebuffer _fill paths.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Continue backend architecture cleanup with domain-specific partial classes and backend infrastructure splits (#328)

* Initial plan

* refactor: split Query.cs into partial class files by domain

Extract 3 domain sections from the 4219-line Query.cs into separate
partial class files:

- Query.Rankings.cs: Player/company rankings, company management,
  game state (GetRankings, GetCompanyRankings, GetMyCompanies,
  GetCompanyBrands, GetCompanySettings, GetGameState, GetStarterIndustries)

- Query.Building.cs: Building lots, exchange, inventory, analytics,
  ledger, power grid, first-sale mission, media houses, marketing stats

- Query.Lending.cs: Bank lending marketplace (loan offers, loans,
  procurement preview, sourcing candidates, unit upgrade info)

Query.cs retains auth/admin/news/stock-exchange, world data/encyclopedia,
and result type classes. Query.Chat.cs remains unchanged.

No logic changes — pure file reorganization.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: scholtz <1223439+scholtz@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: split Mutation.cs into domain-specific partial class files

Extract building operations, lending, and stock exchange mutations from
the 3455-line Mutation.cs into separate partial class files:

- Mutation.Building.cs (993 lines): StoreBuildingConfiguration,
  CancelBuildingConfiguration, SetBuildingForSale, SetRentPerSqm,
  PurchaseLot, CompleteFirstSaleMilestone, UpdatePublicSalesPrice,
  FlushStorage, ScheduleUnitUpgrade, plus building validation helpers
  and FlushStorageResult/FlushStorageEntry result types.

- Mutation.Lending.cs (397 lines): PublishLoanOffer, UpdateLoanOffer,
  DeactivateLoanOffer, AcceptLoan.

- Mutation.StockExchange.cs (421 lines): BuyShares, SellShares, plus
  share pricing snapshot, trading account resolution, shareholding
  management, and ownership ratio helpers.

- Mutation.cs (1699 lines): Auth, admin, news, company management,
  onboarding, and shared private helpers (GetCurrentTickAsync,
  AddCompanyLedgerEntry, PrepareLotPurchaseAsync, etc.).

All 719 backend tests pass. Pure refactor with no behavioral changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: scholtz <1223439+scholtz@users.noreply.github.com>

* Split large backend GraphQL types into domain-specific partial classes

perf: borrowed-frame for CallMethod (Python method calls)

Extend the borrowed-frame pattern to CallMethod handler. When calling
a simple Python method (is_simple + matching arg count), take the
method object from the stack and borrow its Arc fields via ptr::read
instead of cloning 3 Arcs + 1 builtins Arc.

commit.message:
feat: add pre-spawn intelligence to orchestrator

Add phase-specific context gathering before spawning each subagent:

- Implementer: auto-detect coding conventions (.editorconfig, eslint,
  prettier, etc.), tech stack, existing patterns; ask user on first run
- Reviewer: discover review checklists, use code-review agent type
  for platform-enhanced analysis, collect CI/linter results
- Tester: detect test framework and coverage config
- Designer: scan for architecture docs and ADRs

Update agent profiles:
- Reviewer: accept checklist, conventions, CI results in input contract;
  add checklist results to output template
- Implementer: accept coding conventions and existing patterns;
  match project style in Phase 1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(ui): two-tab economics panel (Daily / All-Time)

Daily tab: GDP (with trend arrow), extraction %, efficiency %, tax rate,
today's exports/imports/trade-balance/trade-revenue, money printed today,
sanitation, hospital status.

All-Time tab: tax pool (coloured by health), cumulative taxes collected,
money supply, inflation %, total money ever printed, total exports/imports,
cumulative trade revenue, peak GDP, research points, discoveries count.

Data layer:
- types.ts: 6 new WorldState fields — total_taxes_collected,
  total_money_printed, total_trade_revenue, total_exports, total_imports,
  peak_gdp — all optional so existing save-state survives with ?? 0
- world-init.ts: initialise all 6 fields to 0 (both init paths)
- economy.ts: accumulate into cumulative fields on every daily tick
  (applyExternalTradeBalance, maybePrintEmergencyMoney, applyIncomeTax,
  applyPropertyTax)
- index.ts / macro.ts: update peak_gdp after computeMacroStats each day

CSS: #econ-tabs + .econ-tab/.econ-pane mirror chronicle-tab pattern

commit.message:
tui: fix input newline keys, text color, keyboard enhancement

- Alt+Enter / Ctrl+J as newline keys (work on all terminals without kitty protocol)
- Shift+Enter still works when terminal supports it
- Enable kitty keyboard enhancement (PushKeyboardEnhancementFlags) gracefully
  so Shift+Enter works on supporting terminals (e.g. iTerm2, WezTerm, foot)
- Fix input text color: Color::Indexed(15) = ANSI bright white (works universally,
  unlike Color::Rgb which requires 24-bit terminal support)
- Update hint bar to show Alt+↵/Ctrl+J as the newline shortcut

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: vitals history + clickable stat tiles + duplicate admission prevention

- Fix vitals not showing in history: JSONB vitals_log returned as JS array
  by pg driver, JSON.parse(array) was silently failing. Added parseJsonbArray()
  helper that handles both array and string forms.
- Stat tiles now clickable — click any tile to filter patient list to that
  status. Click again or click Total Occupied to clear filter. Active tile
  shows filled color + 'Filtered' label (same UX as HospitalWorkflow).
- Duplicate admission prevention: admitPatient() now checks for existing
  active admission before INSERT — returns HTTP 409 with clear message.
- handleVitalsSubmit: added vitalsSubmitting state, ⏳ loading button,
  proper catch block that surfaces error in red banner (was empty catch).
- Applied parseJsonbArray() to medications JSONB field in history modal too.

commit.message:
feat: add AI image generation to recipe image upload (#347)

Adds useAIImageGeneration hook and integrates a '✨ Generate image'
button into the RecipeFormSteps image upload zone.

- useAIImageGeneration hook manages loading/success/error state
- Button disabled when recipe name is empty
- Shows 'Regenerate image' label when image already set
- Loading spinner during generation
- On success: sets generated image as recipe imagePreview

Closes theandiman/recipe-management#41

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: borrowed-frame optimization for simple function calls

Skip Arc::clone/drop for code/globals/builtins/constant_cache when
calling simple functions. Instead, bitwise-copy Arcs from the function
object and keep it alive in held_func. Saves 8 atomic operations per
non-recursive function call.

- Added held_func: Option<PyObjectRef> field to Frame
- Added Frame::new_borrowed() constructor using ptr::read
- Updated recycle() to handle held_func before ManuallyDrop
- Updated CallFunction and LoadGlobalCallFunction handlers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Atuin config with inline_height limited to 7

Manages Atuin configuration via chezmoi. Key settings:
- inline_height = 7 to reduce autocomplete clutter
- fuzzy search mode
- secrets filtering enabled
